config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak sewenang-wenang. Dari pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan keputusan yang merugikan orang kecil. Yang lebih tepat adalah mencari keseimbangan: masyarakat dilindungi, tetapi hak dasar individu tetap dihormati dan tidak bisa dikorbankan sembarangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to justify overreach by governments and majorities. I do think there are times when public safety or the common good justifies limits on individual freedom, but those limits should be narrow, lawful, and clearly necessary. The rights of the individual are not a selfish obstacle to society; they are one of the main reasons society is worth trusting in the first place. If we make “society” the default priority, it becomes too easy to ignore minorities and excuse abuse in the name of the greater good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, jadi bagi saya hak individu justru harus jadi pagar agar mayoritas dan negara tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi pembatasan hak hanya sah kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak merampas hak dasar. Kalau tidak, istilah “masyarakat” bisa berubah jadi alat untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, once you say “the common good” automatically comes first, you hand too much power to governments and majorities, and that has historically led to real abuse of minorities and unpopular people. That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every case, because public safety and basic order sometimes require limits, but those limits need to be narrow and justified, not a blank check. In my view, a society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the default, not as something to be overridden whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, istilah “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai pembenaran untuk menekan warga kecil, jadi kalau hak individu tidak jadi batas yang jelas, penyalahgunaan kuasa akan mudah terjadi. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik yang bisa membatasi kebebasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American point of view, that framing is dangerous because “society” is often used to justify government overreach, majority rule, or treating unpopular people as expendable. I do think there are limited cases where public safety or emergency needs justify restricting some rights, but those limits have to be narrow, specific, and tightly accountable. To me, a society is not truly serving the common good if it can only do so by weakening the basic rights that protect each person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
